launchSequence: PRE-LAUNCH GATE (weeks 1-3, engineering-first): Nothing goes public until strangers can log in without breaking each other. Ship in this order: (a) Auth.js credentials signup with invite-code gating (roadmap #20), (b) rebuild slice S5 — per-socket userId + MT5 WS ownership scoping (the current unscoped broadcast is a hard security blocker for multi-user), (c) slice S3 workspace autosave (a beta user who loses their workspace on refresh churns on day 1), (d) deploy API+web to one cheap VPS (Hetzner/DO ~$20/mo) behind a domain + Caddy TLS — the laptop cannot be the host the moment invite #1 goes out. Marketing work runs in parallel but publishes nothing until this gate closes.

PRE-LAUNCH ASSET 1 — PulseScript public docs (same weeks, low-effort/high-leverage): Convert docs/pulsescript-language.md + pulsescript-design.md into a public docs site (Starlight or Nextra on the same domain, static, zero cost). This is required for beta anyway, it is the channel's best SEO surface ('Pine Script alternative' searches), and it is proof-of-seriousness in every video description. Include a 'PulseScript for Pine users' translation page — the audience already writes Pine.

PRE-LAUNCH ASSET 2 — Waitlist (day the docs go live): One landing page: 60-90s real screen capture of the terminal (multi-pane, footprint, PulseScript run, Telegram alert arriving on a phone), email capture via a free tier (Tally/Buttondown), and — primary, because the audience is Telegram-native — a public 'SuperCharts Beta' Telegram channel as the waitlist. One required question on the form: 'What is your #1 TradingView frustration, and do you trade on MT5?' — this both sharpens positioning copy and ranks beta invitees.

PRE-LAUNCH ANNOUNCE — Video V1 'I built my own TradingView' (build-story video, see channelPlan): waitlist link pinned, plus a community-tab post and edited pinned comments on the channel's top 5 videos by lifetime traffic pointing at the waitlist. Target: 300-500 waitlist joins before beta opens.

PRIVATE BETA (weeks 4-7, 20-30 users): Hand-pick from the waitlist — prioritize MT5 answers and long-time commenters. Onboard through a private Telegram group (not email): personal invite code, a 5-step 'first hour' checklist (load a symbol → save a layout → run the sample PulseScript → create one EMA-cross alert → receive it in Telegram). Run one 45-min office-hours livestream per week (doubles as channel content). Feedback loop with a mechanism: bug reported → fixed → user named in the changelog message posted to the group; this is what makes 25 traders evangelize.

DURING BETA — ship the two explicit public-launch asks, with beta users as spec: (a) Scanner v1 — replace the Binance-top-movers stub with a real server-side screener over the 48-symbol catalog reusing @supercharts/indicators computeAll (filter rows: RSI level, MA cross state, % change, RVOL; saved presets; click-through to chart). Beta group votes on the first 5 preset scans. (b) Indicator coverage wave — start with the already-punchlisted INC-10 set (DEMA/TEMA/VWMA) plus whatever beta users ask for by name. Do NOT demo or mention the scanner publicly until (a) is real — the working agreement's no-fake rule applies to marketing too.

MONETIZATION GATE (week 7-8): Turn Stripe live (roadmap #21) with exactly two SKUs ($400/6mo, $600/12mo) + a 14-day full-access trial, no card required. Before public beta, offer beta users a founding-member deal (e.g. 50% off their first term, locked 'founder' badge, capped at 30). Every founding purchase is willingness-to-pay validation; if fewer than ~5 of 25 active beta users buy at half price, fix the product or pricing before going public — do not launch past that signal.

PUBLIC BETA (week 8-9): Open signups (trial-gated, no invite codes), publish Video V2 (the MT5 automation demo — the strongest differentiator, see channelPlan), docs site indexed, /s/<token> public strategy-share pages get a signup CTA banner (they already exist and are the product's only viral surface — every shared backtest is now a landing page). Status page + a visible 'beta' label so honesty stays intact.

LAUNCH WEEK (week 10): Three videos in seven days (V3 PulseScript end-to-end, V4 scanner, V5 order-flow — pre-recorded during beta) + one live 'first live-market session on SuperCharts' stream. Same-week zero-budget seeding, each with a real artifact not a product pitch: r/algotrading post = the PulseScript walk-forward results with methodology; Forex Factory thread = the MT5-bridge architecture writeup; X/Twitter thread = the 12-screenshot build story. Personal Telegram/email to every waitlist member who has not signed up: 'beta is open, here is your link'. Founding-member price expires at end of launch week (real deadline, announced in V1).

POST-LAUNCH (permanent operating mode, ~30% of weekly time): (1) Every future channel video — strategy breakdowns, market analysis, EA content — is recorded INSIDE SuperCharts; the product becomes the channel's b-roll, which is the only sustainable zero-budget distribution a solo founder has. (2) Weekly 'PulseScript of the week' segment: write a viewer-requested strategy live, backtest it, share the /s/ link in the description. (3) Trial-expiry sequence: day 10 personal email + a Telegram message summarizing what THEIR alerts caught during the trial (the product writes the retention pitch). (4) Weekly 30-min metrics review against the instrumented numbers; one growth experiment per week maximum, so engineering slices S2/S4/S6-S8 keep landing.
channelPlan: Positioning for every video: 'trader who builds his own tools' — never 'startup founder launching an app'. The channel's existing MT5/forex algo audience is the exact ICP, and the demos must only show what is real (the repo's no-fabrication rule extends to content). Cadence a solo founder can hold: one long video every 2 weeks pre-launch, three in launch week (pre-recorded during beta), then a weekly segment inside normal trading content. Every video: same pinned CTA, per-video UTM link so conversion is attributable. Cut a 30-60s Short from each long video around the single wow-moment. VIDEO SLATE — V1 (pre-launch announce): 'I got tired of TradingView, so I spent a year building my own' — build story, honest about what it can't do yet, waitlist CTA; story videos outperform feature videos for warm audiences. V2 (public-beta lead, the killer demo): 'My charts now trade my MT5 account' — full flow on a live demo account: build a signal in the strategy builder → walk-forward it → flip on the MT5 bridge → watch the order hit MetaTrader 5. This is the one thing the audience cannot do in TradingView without paying for third-party webhook glue, and no competitor video exists for it. V3 (launch week): 'I invented a scripting language for my charts' — PulseScript end-to-end in one unbroken take: write an EMA-cross strategy in the editor (syntax highlighting on screen) → backtest + optimizer + walk-forward stats → one click to a live alert → phone on camera receiving the Telegram message with the chart snapshot PNG. The compressed 'idea to alerted strategy in 4 minutes' cut is the Short. V4 (launch week): 'Scanning 48 crypto + forex pairs in real time' — scanner v1 finding setups live, click-through to the chart, save the preset; only publishable once scanner v1 is shipped. V5 (launch week): 'Free order-flow: footprint, DOM and liquidity heatmap in the browser' — Binance order-flow suite; explicitly honest that it is Binance-only (the honesty is itself differentiating in this niche). V6 (post-launch trust play): 'TradingView vs my own terminal — honest comparison from someone who built one' — includes the gaps; this video earns the credibility that converts skeptics, and comparison keywords bring cold search traffic. Ongoing: weekly office-hours livestreams during beta (feedback + content in one time slot) and the 'PulseScript of the week' recurring segment with the /s/ share link in every description.
firstHundred: Arithmetic first, tactics second: 100 signups from a warm channel audience does not need virality, it needs three stacked mechanisms. (1) HAND-RECRUITED 30 — before anything public, personally invite ~30 people by name: the channel's most frequent commenters, anyone who has ever emailed bluealgocapital about EAs or tools, MT5 clients if any. One-to-one Telegram/email message ('I built this, you are one of 30 people I want breaking it'), personal invite code each. These 30 are guaranteed, and they seed the beta Telegram group that everything else feeds into. (2) VIDEO FUNNEL ~40 — V1 waitlist video + community post + pinned comments on the top-5 historical videos drive the waitlist (target 300-500 joins at a realistic 1-3% of views); at public beta, a personal 'beta is open' message to every waitlist member converts warm intent — expect 30-50 signups from the waitlist plus launch-week videos V2-V5, with per-video UTM links proving which angle converts (double down on that angle post-launch). (3) ACTIVATION-GATED REFERRAL ~30 — each beta/early user unlocks exactly 3 invite codes only AFTER their first saved artifact (layout, script, or alert), so codes sit with activated users, not tourists; 30 activated users × 3 codes at even 30% redemption ≈ 30 signups, and the mechanism doubles as an activation incentive. FORCE MULTIPLIER already in the repo: the public /s/<token> strategy-share pages — add a signup CTA to them and make sharing a backtest the natural end of every PulseScript-of-the-week segment; every share a user posts into their own trading Telegram groups is a zero-cost landing page. Explicitly out of scope for the first 100: paid ads (none), cold outreach to strangers, and Product Hunt (wrong audience for a $400 trading terminal — the niche forums in launch week are the equivalent). Timeline expectation: 30 at private beta open, cumulative 100 within 2-3 weeks of public beta if the waitlist hits ~300; if the waitlist stalls under 150, the fix is another V1-style story video, not more channels.
metrics: 1. SIGNUPS BY SOURCE — weekly count of completed Auth.js signups, tagged by UTM (per-video links, referral code, /s/ share page, docs site, direct). Instrument: server-side event on the signup route + a source column; this is the only way to know which video angle actually converts.

2. ACTIVATION RATE — % of new signups who create their FIRST SAVED ARTIFACT (saved layout, saved PulseScript via user_scripts, or created alert — whichever comes first) within 48h of signup. All three already pass through Fastify routes, so this is one server-side event, not client analytics. Target: >40% during beta; below that, fix the first-hour checklist before spending on acquisition.

3. WEEK-1 RETENTION — % of activated users active on ≥2 distinct days in days 2-7 after signup ('active' = an authenticated API session, trivially derivable once auth lands). This is the number that decides whether public launch is warranted; a leading sub-signal worth watching alongside it: % of active users with ≥1 live alert configured, because the Telegram alert is the hook that pulls traders back.

4. TRIAL→PAID CONVERSION — % of trials converting via Stripe within 21 days of signup, split by plan ($400/6mo vs $600/12mo) and by source. During private beta the proxy is founding-member purchases (<5 of ~25 active beta users buying at 50% off = do not launch publicly yet).

5. ALERTS DELIVERED PER ACTIVE USER PER WEEK — Telegram + web deliveries from the alert engine, per active user (the engine already logs deliveries; add the per-user rollup). This is the product's stickiness engine and the earliest churn predictor: a user whose alert count drops to zero is a cancellation you can still save with one personal message. INSTRUMENTATION NOTE for all five: one `events` table in the existing node:sqlite DB (event, user_id, source, ts) + a weekly SQL query — no third-party analytics needed at this scale; add self-hosted Umami on the landing/docs pages only for top-of-funnel page views.
